response:
## /decide run complete — ADR 0036 accepted

**Process followed:** Inherited context (no git remote in this sandbox, so no open PRs/branches to reconcile — noted, not a gap); framed the scope (proceed/decline/conditions on the Issue #109 routing experiment); ran the project's advisor panel fresh and parallel (process-economist, lean-process-engineer, session-operator — all three independently converged on "accept, but gate tightly," with session-operator surfacing the sharpest tension: no consumption point exists for a winning verdict); PM weighed them and wrote the ADR; a fresh, uncontaminated verifier reproduced every load-bearing claim against real repo state and returned **PASS, no blockers**. No safety-BLOCKER caveat was folded in, so red-team/tech-lead weren't triggered. Written to `docs/decisions/0036-routing-experiment-gate.md` (5,902/6,000 chars).

One process note for the record: `node pdca-workflow/scripts/adr-lint.mjs` was sandbox-blocked for both me and the verifier ("requires approval") — the verifier hand-reproduced the linter's full decision logic against the file instead of guessing a result. You may want to run the linter yourself before merge to get a machine exit code.

**Bottom line: proceed — but only with the cheap checker-fidelity pilot, not the full grid, and adoption is double-gated (pilot clears the false-accept floor *and* something actually exists to consume a winning config).**

---

```markdown
---
id: 0036
title: "routing cascade experiment: run the checker-fidelity pilot only; double-gate the rest"
status: accepted
summary: "Issue #109 proposes 'routing' (haiku attempts once, checker accepts-or-escalates, sonnet redoes the whole task on escalate) — a mechanism #41's grid never populated (0035 rejected iterative coach-retry, not this). Authorize ONLY the checker-fidelity pilot: checker calls against the 24 retained haiku-solo artifacts, no new task execution, pre-registered kill gate (no checker beats ~10-15% false-accept -> null). Do NOT pre-commit to an adopt/full arm: 0035:18 adds no escalation harness and none exists (static frontmatter, decide/SKILL.md:43), so a positive verdict has nowhere to land — it is double-gated on (a) pilot clears the floor AND (b) a real consumption point exists."
---

# 0036 — routing cascade experiment: pilot only, double-gate the rest

- Date: 2026-07-12
- Owner: PM
- Panel: process-economist (is the pilot cheap / the grid worth funding), lean-process-engineer (muda vs poka-yoke — distinct arm or #41 sunk-cost; don't gold-plate the harness), session-operator (does a positive result have anywhere to land). This subset: the call is a spend-vs-information tradeoff hinging on a landing-pad question; no consumer-adoption or plugin-design lens bears, since 0035 already closed the plugin door.
- Context: Issue #109 proposes a follow-up to #41 — "routing": haiku attempts a task once, a checker decides accept-or-escalate, sonnet redoes the WHOLE task (no coaching) on escalate. #41's rejected mechanism was iterative coach-and-retry (0035:16); routing removes iteration entirely — a mechanistically distinct arm the #41 grid never populated. Open question: run it, and under what conditions?

## Decision
Priority-ordered:
1. **Run the checker-fidelity pilot only.** Reuse the hermetic executor + blind grade (ADR 0019/0023/0025) verbatim. Spend = checker calls against the 24 retained `haiku-solo` artifacts in `benchmarks/2026-07-10-tiered-execution-fullgrid/outputs/` (verified present) — zero new task execution. Compare >=2 checker variants (sonnet judge AND a deterministic checklist checker, the latter untested in #41).
2. **Pre-register the kill gate BEFORE any checker call** (ADR 0024 D3, no post-hoc bar move): if no checker variant beats ~10-15% false-accept on the retained artifacts, record the null, stop. #41's 25% sonnet-validator false-accept (0035:27) makes clearing this a priori unlikely — which is precisely why this is the first and possibly only spend.
3. **Do NOT authorize an adopt/"full grid" arm on pilot success alone — double-gate it:** (a) pilot clears the floor AND (b) a real consumption point exists — a live-or-committed dynamic per-task escalation mechanism a winning routing config plugs into. None exists today: 0035:18 adds no worker/escalation agent and every agent selects model statically (`decide/SKILL.md:43`). Absent (b), a positive routing verdict is a finding on a shelf; record it, don't build to it.
4. **There is barely a grid to fund.** The existing `haiku-solo` and `sonnet-solo` cells ARE routing's two halves; routing cost/quality compose arithmetically from them + the measured checker false-accept rate (ISSUE-109.md:10). So the only new spend is checker calls (the pilot) + composition — dissolving the scope-creep risk of a "full grid" run on hope.

## Justification
Cost x risk x value. Pilot: low cost (checker calls only; artifacts verified retained), low risk (pre-registered kill gate + double-gate on landing), moderate-conditional value — mechanistically new (0035 never populated single-shot escalation), and even a null closes routing under current checkers, a real finding. Adopting on pilot success alone has no landing pad (0035:18; no harness) — that is the whole reason to gate, not the reason to decline. Building the harness first to create a landing pad inverts ADR 0024 (build the consumer before the mechanism earns it) — declined as gold-plating.

## Assumptions
- [checkable] The 24 retained `haiku-solo` artifacts (+ `haiku-solo.summary.json`) are valid checker-fidelity inputs without re-running haiku — no task/rubric drift since 2026-07-10. Artifacts confirmed present; drift check vs `benchmarks/2026-07-10-tiered-execution-fullgrid/metadata.json` — owner: pilot gate, before spend. **(weakest link — the pilot's whole cheapness rests here.)**
- [checkable-doc] No live or planned dynamic per-task escalation harness exists to consume a routing verdict — PM-verified: `decide/SKILL.md:43` (frontmatter `model:` is the tier SSoT) + 0035:18; grep for routing/escalation returns only skill-doc references, no orchestration mechanism.
- [checkable] A checker (sonnet judge or deterministic checklist) beats ~10-15% false-accept on the retained artifacts — owner: pilot; result: pending (measured by the pilot this ADR authorizes; the gate cannot run it now). This IS the falsifiable kill criterion.

## Rejected alternatives
- Authorize the full/adopt arm now or on pilot success alone — no consumption point (0035:18); a positive verdict can't land, and it funds spend on hope (the panel's flagged scope-creep risk).
- Decline the pilot entirely — forgoes cheap information on a mechanistically new arm #41 never populated; spend is only checker calls on retained artifacts, and even a null is a real finding.
- Build the escalation harness first to create a landing pad — premature; inverts ADR 0024 (measure the mechanism before building its consumer).

## Revisit triggers
- Pilot measures a checker under ~10-15% false-accept -> compose the routing cost/quality estimate; re-decide adopt, still gated on a consumption point.
- A dynamic per-task escalation harness becomes real or committed (reopening 0035:18) -> routing gains a landing pad; re-test adopt at that point.
- Haiku/Sonnet pricing or checker capability shifts materially -> re-run the pilot.
```

The PM also flagged pre-existing, unrelated `adr-lint` debt (an unpointed ADR 0026→0023 back-cite) discovered incidentally — left untouched per "one concern per PR"; worth a separate small fix before your next merge.
